notes: All 8 steps completed. Key discovery: SolidJS createEffect runs during completeUpdates after createRoot callback exits, not inside it. Tests must extract dispose/mutators from createRoot and run assertions outside. Implementation tracks prevSelected/prevHovered to detect selection-only changes for immediate dispatch vs hover debounce.

summary: Write failing test: selection-only change calls invoke immediately (0ms), not after 100ms debounce. Mock @tauri-apps/api/core with vi.mock, use vi.useFakeTimers. In a createRoot, create the store, call selectEntity('Bracket'), then verify mockInvoke was called with 'update_selection' and { selectedEntity: 'Bracket', hoveredEntity: null } synchronously (before advancing timers).; Write failing test: hover-only change calls invoke only after 100ms debounce. Create store, call hoverEntity('Bracket.width'), assert mockInvoke NOT called, advance timers by 100ms, assert mockInvoke called once with { selectedEntity: null, hoveredEntity: 'Bracket.width' }.; Write failing test: combined selection+hover change is debounced at 100ms. Create store, call selectEntity('Bracket') then hoverEntity('Bracket.width') in same tick, assert invoke not called until 100ms. This tests that when hover changes alongside selection, we use the debounce path.; Write failing test: immediate selection dispatch cancels pending debounced hover. Create store, hoverEntity('X') (starts 100ms timer), then before timer fires selectEntity('Bracket') — the selection invoke fires immediately, and when 100ms passes only one additional invoke fires (not the stale hover-only one). Verify total invoke calls and argument correctness.; Implement immediate selection dispatch in selectionStore.ts. Track prevSelected and prevHovered outside the effect. Inside the effect: (1) always clear pending timer, (2) check if only selectedEntity changed (selectedEntity !== prevSelected && hoveredEntity === prevHovered), (3) if selection-only → call invoke synchronously, (4) else → debounce at 100ms, (5) update prevSelected/prevHovered after dispatching. Fix comments to accurately describe the dual-path behavior.; Write test: rapid hover changes collapse into single invoke at 100ms (existing debounce behavior preserved). Create store, hoverEntity('A'), advance 50ms, hoverEntity('B'), advance 100ms. Assert invoke called once with hoveredEntity='B'.; Write test: cleanup disposes the debounce timer. Create store in createRoot, hoverEntity('X'), then dispose the root. Advance timers by 200ms. Assert invoke was NOT called (the onCleanup cleared the pending timer).; Write test: invoke rejection is silently caught (no unhandled promise). Create store, mockInvoke.mockRejectedValue(new Error('not in Tauri')), selectEntity('Bracket'). Assert no error thrown.

BLOCKED: vite-plugin-solid 2.11.11 + Vite 6 marks solid-js as test.server.deps.external, causing Node.js to resolve dist/server.js where createEffect is a no-op. All 5 backend-sync tests fail (invoke never called). Verified fix: add test.server.deps.inline: [/solid-js/] to gui/vitest.config.ts. Escalated as esc-592-16 requesting scope expansion or direct fix on main.

Resumed after escalation fix (vitest server.deps.conditions). Step 9: Fixed combined selection+hover test to use SolidJS batch() — single effect run with both changes, genuinely testing debounce path. Step 10: Added sequential behavior test documenting immediate selection dispatch then debounced hover invoke. All 22 selectionStore tests pass, all 855 GUI tests pass. All 10 plan steps complete.
